articles.title: NASA’s Changing Stance on UFOs: Full Timeline
articles.summary: NASA's relationship with UFOs has evolved alongside broader changes in public and government interest in unexplained sightings. Newsweek spoke with experts about how the public's and scientific community's views have evolved over time. As new UFO—now commonly referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs)—reports, official investigations and congressional hearings have brought the topic back into the spotlight, the agency has increasingly emphasized a scientific approach to studying the phenomenon. Former NASA official and UAP study team member Michael Gold recently argued that the topic was not always viewed through the same lens.

en.title: NASA’s Changing Stance on UFOs: Full Timeline
en.summary: NASA's relationship with UFOs has evolved alongside broader changes in public and government interest in unexplained sightings. Newsweek spoke with experts about how the public's and scientific community's views have evolved over time. As new UFO—now commonly referred to as unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs)—reports, official investigations and congressional hearings have brought the topic back into the spotlight, the agency has increasingly emphasized a scientific approach to studying the phenomenon.

de.title: Die sich ändernde Haltung der NASA zu UFOs: Vollständige Zeitleiste
de.summary: Die Beziehung der NASA zu UFOs hat sich im Zuge umfassenderer Veränderungen im öffentlichen und staatlichen Interesse an ungeklärten Sichtungen weiterentwickelt. Newsweek sprach mit Experten darüber, wie sich die Ansichten der Öffentlichkeit und der wissenschaftlichen Gemeinschaft im Laufe der Zeit entwickelt haben. Da neue UFO-Berichte, offizielle Untersuchungen und Anhörungen vor dem Kongress das Thema wieder ins Rampenlicht gerückt haben – heute allgemein als nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) bezeichnet – hat die Agentur zunehmend Wert auf einen wissenschaftlichen Ansatz bei der Untersuchung des Phänomens gelegt.

articles.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
articles.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels. Representatives Eric Burlison, Jared Moskowitz, Anna Paulina Luna, and Tim Burchett are expected to attend, while Kean and Fox will host the gathering. Grusch’s testimony has already moved the UAP topic from internet speculation to sworn congressional testimony, and the Capitol event aims to push for the review and public release of any existing classified records, subject to national‑security safeguards. The organizers argue that if such files exist, the issue shifts from public curiosity to whether intelligence agencies or contractors have operated beyond congressional oversight, and that secrecy cannot be a permanent shield for unlawful concealment or obstruction of Congress.

en.title: David Grusch Returns To Capitol Steps As UFO Disclosure Fight Moves From Testimony To Action
en.summary: On June 9, 2026, former intelligence officer David Grusch is scheduled to speak on the U.S. Capitol steps with filmmaker James Fox, author Leslie Kean, and several bipartisan representatives to demand the release of UFO and UAP files. The event is framed as a call for declassification, stronger whistleblower protections, and new disclosure legislation, with Grusch planning to discuss evidence of non‑human intelligence he claims to have seen through classified channels.

de.title: David Grusch kehrt zu den Stufen des Kapitols zurück, während der UFO-Enthüllungskampf von der Aussage zur Tat übergeht
de.summary: Am 9. Juni 2026 soll der ehemalige Geheimdienstoffizier David Grusch auf den Stufen des US-Kapitols mit dem Filmemacher James Fox, dem Autor Leslie Kean und mehreren überparteilichen Vertretern sprechen, um die Freigabe von UFO- und UAP-Dateien zu fordern. Die Veranstaltung ist als Aufruf zur Freigabe, einem stärkeren Whistleblower-Schutz und einer neuen Offenlegungsgesetzgebung angelegt, wobei Grusch plant, Beweise für nichtmenschliche Geheimdienste zu diskutieren, die er angeblich über geheime Kanäle eingesehen hat.

articles.title: Stream Disclosure Day (2026) online at home starting next week!
articles.summary: Steven Spielberg’s science‑fiction film *Disclosure Day* will be available for digital purchase on Prime Video beginning July 21, 2026, with a release time of 1:00 AM EDT. The film is offered as a UHD pre‑order for $29.99, and Amazon’s Pre‑order Price Guarantee ensures customers pay the lowest listed price between ordering and release. The cast includes Emily Blunt, Josh O’Connor, Colin Firth, Eve Hewson and Colman Domingo, and the screenplay is by David Koepp. After a theatrical run earlier in 2026 that earned $233 million worldwide against a $115 million budget, the movie has sparked widespread discussion among science‑fiction fans. The synopsis poses the question, “If you found out we weren’t alone, would that frighten you?” and describes a world changed by the revelation of extraterrestrial life. The article notes that further home‑media releases such as Blu‑ray, 4K UHD or streaming subscription availability will be announced if they become available.

articles.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
articles.summary: Mike Gold, a former NASA UAP study member, says officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material beyond the previously released “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold told Ross Coultart on Newsnight that the National Archives are shifting toward declassifying material that remains classified and that bureaucracy is slowing the process. He argues that current disclosures focus too much on military and Earth‑based sightings and urges investigators to examine NASA’s space‑based archives, noting that no data from space assets has yet been released. Gold expects the forthcoming release to contain more sensitive material and believes it could be “even more interesting” than earlier tranches. Reddit users reacted to Gold’s comments, with some expressing optimism about the progress and others remaining skeptical after earlier releases lacked new evidence.

en.title: NASA insider hints bigger UFO file release as declassification ramps up
en.summary: Mike Gold, a former NASA UAP study member, says officials are discussing declassifying still‑secret UFO material beyond the previously released “low‑hanging fruit.” Gold told Ross Coultart on Newsnight that the National Archives are shifting toward declassifying material that remains classified and that bureaucracy is slowing the process. He argues that current disclosures focus too much on military and Earth‑based sightings and urges investigators to examine NASA’s space‑based archives, noting that no data from space assets has yet been released.

de.title: NASA-Insider deuten auf eine größere Veröffentlichung von UFO-Dateien hin, da die Freigabe voranschreitet
de.summary: Mike Gold, ein ehemaliges NASA-UAP-Studienmitglied, sagt, dass Beamte über die Freigabe von noch geheimem UFO-Material diskutieren, das über die zuvor veröffentlichten „Low-Hanging Fruits“ hinausgeht. Gold sagte Ross Coultart auf Newsnight, dass die Nationalarchive dazu übergehen, weiterhin geheim gehaltenes Material freizugeben, und dass die Bürokratie den Prozess verlangsame. Er argumentiert, dass sich die aktuellen Offenlegungen zu sehr auf militärische und erdbasierte Sichtungen konzentrieren, und fordert die Ermittler dringend auf, die weltraumgestützten Archive der NASA zu untersuchen, wobei er darauf hinweist, dass noch keine Daten von Weltraumanlagen veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Trump UFO advisors have explanations for glowing orbs — except for one recent encounter
articles.summary: Trump administration science advisors are providing conventional explanations for some UFO sightings but say one recent encounter remains unexplained. Dr. Avi Loeb, head of the UAP Science Advisory Council, argues that many glowing orbs could be plasmas produced by powerful lasers, which can move supersonically without generating a sonic boom or fireball. Loeb notes that these laser‑based explanations cannot account for the October 2023 sighting of orange and red orbs near a secured military facility in the western United States, which were observed by federal intelligence officers. The All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) report, referenced by Loeb, describes an orange “mother orb” that launched smaller red orbs, remained stationary for hours, and exhibited coordinated horizontal motion and altitude changes, a behavior that does not match laser‑sustained plasma. Admiral Tim Gallaudet agrees that the “orbs launching orbs” case is a chief concern and cannot be explained by conventional means, while Loeb also considers ball lighting as a possibility but notes its brief duration compared to the hours‑long sighting.

articles.title: Exclusive: 2 SW Florida cities top 10 for UFO sightings
articles.summary: Naples and Cape Coral are ranked No. 6 and No. 9 respectively among Florida cities for UFO sightings according to data from the National UFO Reporting Center (NUFORC). The NUFORC list, which tracks sightings submitted since 1995, shows Naples with 134 reports and Cape Coral with 117, while other Southwest Florida cities such as Captiva, Bonita Springs, and Fort Myers Beach have 22, 18, and 11 reports respectively. Naples Police Lt. Bryan McGinn said he has not received any service calls about UFO or UAP sightings, indicating that local authorities are not being notified of the reports. UFO experts note that about 5 % of sightings remain unexplained, a figure cited by journalist Leslie Kean, who has written on the subject and reported on a $22 million U.S. Department of Defense program investigating UFOs. Recent reports on NUFORC include a 2026 sighting in Port Charlotte of lights descending from the sky, a 2025 March report of a possible balloon in Naples, and a March 22, 2025 sighting of a black, shapeshifting object hovering over Green Cove Springs near Jacksonville. The article does not confirm whether any of these sightings were linked to rocket launches or drones, leaving the true nature of the objects uncertain.

articles.title: These 20 Tennessee cities report the most UFO sightings. Where yours ranks
articles.summary: Since the 2023 National Defense Authorization Act, U.S. reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) have risen, with the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) reviewing over 1,000 cases but finding no evidence of extraterrestrial technology. UFO investigator Mick West, who has examined roughly 1,000 cases and analyzed about 100 in depth, advises a methodical approach that cross‑checks sightings with flight data, weather, ground cameras, and satellite imagery to rule out mundane explanations. West recommends using tools such as Invisor to extract metadata from videos, FlightRadar24 to view contemporaneous aircraft positions, and his own Sitrec software to reconstruct the scene with satellite data. AARO accepts UAP reports only from official sources like the military and FAA, while civilians can share their footage on databases such as Enigma and MUFON for community analysis. The article notes that while most anomalies have logical explanations, some remain unexplained and could potentially be the first true alien encounter, though this remains uncertain.

UFO investigator Mick West, who has examined roughly 1,000 cases and analyzed about 100 in depth, advises a methodical approach that cross‑checks sightings with flight data, weather, ground cameras, and satellite imagery to rule out mundane explanations.

de.title: Diese 20 Städte in Tennessee melden die meisten UFO-Sichtungen. Wo Ihr Rang liegt
de.summary: Seit dem National Defense Authorization Act von 2023 sind in den USA die Meldungen über nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene (UAPs) gestiegen. Das All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office (AARO) hat über 1.000 Fälle geprüft, aber keine Hinweise auf außerirdische Technologie gefunden. Der UFO-Ermittler Mick West, der rund 1.000 Fälle untersucht und etwa 100 eingehend analysiert hat, rät zu einem methodischen Ansatz, bei dem Sichtungen mit Flugdaten, Wetter, Bodenkameras und Satellitenbildern abgeglichen werden, um banale Erklärungen auszuschließen.

articles.title: Moon monoliths are Luis Elizondo's latest UFO bombshell
articles.summary: Luis Elizondo, a former Army counterintelligence special agent and long‑time UFO whistleblower, said that unreleased photographs of the Moon show monolithic structures with right‑angle cuts that he believes are artificial and may be released soon. He also described encounters with colored orbs—green, red, and blue—that he claims could be autonomous surveillance or weapon platforms, citing reports from the Pentagon’s former AATIP. The interview contains no new public evidence; the claims rely on photographs and reports that have not yet been made public. The Reddit UFO community reacted skeptically, demanding concrete proof rather than further announcements. Elizondo repeatedly used tentative language such as “appears to be” and noted that CIA, DIA, and NSA officials have concluded the objects are real and potentially threatening, but the existence of the alleged Moon structures remains unverified. He has previously claimed that a secret government program holds alien craft and that UAPs can disable nuclear missiles, but those claims are not substantiated in this interview.

He also described encounters with colored orbs—green, red, and blue—that he claims could be autonomous surveillance or weapon platforms, citing reports from the Pentagon’s former AATIP. The interview contains no new public evidence; the claims rely on photographs and reports that have not yet been made public.

de.title: Mondmonolithen sind Luis Elizondos neueste UFO-Bombe
de.summary: Luis Elizondo, ein ehemaliger Spionageabwehr-Spezialagent der Armee und langjähriger UFO-Whistleblower, sagte, dass unveröffentlichte Fotos des Mondes monolithische Strukturen mit rechtwinkligen Schnitten zeigen, die seiner Meinung nach künstlich sind und möglicherweise bald veröffentlicht werden. Er beschrieb auch Begegnungen mit farbigen Kugeln – grün, rot und blau –, bei denen es sich seiner Meinung nach um autonome Überwachungs- oder Waffenplattformen handeln könnte, und berief sich dabei auf Berichte der früheren AATIP des Pentagons. Das Interview enthält keine neuen öffentlichen Beweise; Die Behauptungen stützen sich auf Fotos und Berichte, die noch nicht veröffentlicht wurden.

articles.title: Roswell UFO Festival organizers share 2026 attendance numbers
articles.summary: The Roswell UFO Festival ran from July 2 to July 4, 2026, and organizers reported it was a success. The event featured a variety of booths, shops, shows, and contests aimed at paranormal enthusiasts. Roswell Mainstreet estimated that more than 12,000 people attended, though those figures are preliminary. No further details about attendance or event outcomes were provided.
